local_sources: Incoming client folders, fiscal documents, invoices, notices, scans and email files
local_processing: Bounded local OCR and text extraction for supported PDF, image, text, DOCX, XLSX and EML content
Bounded SHA-256 source ledger with non-followed symlink records and a final source type/byte-integrity check before sealing
Per-output byte hashing and package sealing after review write-back
Issue a bounded opaque hosted-review persistence binding so Save and Apply can reach the server-held run without exposing its local directory

Remove known local-path fields, known absolute path occurrences, and private QA text from the browser payload served through the loopback workbench, and require a per-session token for write operations

codex_context.purpose: Prepare the studio intake synthesis and document requests
codex_context.content: Generated inventory, structured fields, formal-check results, extraction diagnostics and sanitized run metadata; relative filenames needed for document review; source excerpts, fiscal evidence snippets and generated draft previews only when the operator explicitly enables preview mode
codex_context.minimum_necessary: Keep review previews off by default, remove the explicit client name and absolute workstation paths from the browser/MCP payload, treat relative filenames as potentially identifying, and use generated evidence first. Explicit preview mode adds bounded excerpts from every readable inventoried document, fiscal-field evidence snippets, and bounded previews of the generated studio brief, memo and client email, so enable it only for a deliberately scoped folder.

codex_context.purpose: Draft a reviewable request for missing or unclear evidence
codex_context.content: Missing-item list, case context needed for clarity and selected supporting facts
codex_context.minimum_necessary: Use only facts required to explain the request and do not reproduce unrelated client data

residual_risks.risk: Structured fields and selected passages can contain direct identifiers and fiscal information
residual_risks.mitigation: Keep the complete folder local and limit Codex to extracted results and passages necessary for intake synthesis

residual_risks.risk: An EML body may be readable while its attachments remain unprocessed
residual_risks.mitigation: Record skipped attachments and keep the file unresolved until relevant attachments are supplied as separately inventoried files

residual_risks.risk: The private run intake records absolute local folder and file paths that may themselves identify a client or workstation user
residual_risks.mitigation: Keep run_intake.json owner-only and local, sanitize all browser/MCP review payloads, and consult private path metadata only when needed to resolve a local artifact

residual_risks.risk: A source file or non-followed symlink can change type or content while a run is in progress
residual_risks.mitigation: Bind extraction to the initial bounded source ledger, never extract entries recorded as symlinks, and reject source type, size or SHA-256 drift before sealing

residual_risks.risk: Relative filenames in the review payload can contain client names or other identifiers
residual_risks.mitigation: Retain filenames only because the reviewer must identify each document; remove explicit client-name fields and absolute paths, keep the review surface access-controlled, and rename source files before the run when filename disclosure is not acceptable

residual_risks.risk: A hosted persistence token could be replayed while its short-lived server-side run binding remains valid
residual_risks.mitigation: Use a random opaque token with bounded lifetime and capacity, bind it to the review hash and run identifier, and reject unknown, expired or mismatched tokens without falling back to browser-supplied paths

residual_risks.risk: Explicit preview mode includes bounded excerpts from every readable inventoried document, fiscal evidence snippets and generated draft previews that may repeat the client name or other identifying facts
residual_risks.mitigation: Keep previews disabled by default and enable them only for a deliberately scoped folder after the operator accepts all three bounded preview surfaces

commercialista_notice.message_it: La cartella cliente completa resta locale. La review Codex riceve risultati strutturati e nomi file relativi. Le anteprime sono disattivate: se abilitate esplicitamente, riceve anche estratti limitati di ogni documento leggibile, evidenze dei campi fiscali e bozze generate, che possono ripetere il nome cliente.
commercialista_notice.message_en: The complete client folder remains local. Codex review receives structured results and relative filenames. Previews are off by default; if explicitly enabled, it also receives bounded excerpts from every readable document, fiscal-field evidence, and generated drafts that may repeat the client name.
commercialista_notice.message_fr: Le dossier client complet reste local. La revue Codex reçoit les résultats structurés et les noms de fichiers relatifs. Les aperçus sont désactivés par défaut ; s’ils sont activés explicitement, elle reçoit aussi des extraits limités de chaque document lisible, les preuves des champs fiscaux et les brouillons générés, qui peuvent répéter le nom du client.
commercialista_notice.message_de: Der vollständige Mandantenordner bleibt lokal. Die Codex-Prüfung erhält strukturierte Ergebnisse und relative Dateinamen. Vorschauen sind standardmäßig deaktiviert; bei ausdrücklicher Aktivierung erhält sie außerdem begrenzte Auszüge aus jedem lesbaren Dokument, Nachweise zu Steuerfeldern und erzeugte Entwürfe, die den Mandantennamen wiederholen können.

limitations: Document diversity can require additional passages during professional review, and EML attachments must be inventoried separately
This is a design-time data-flow review, not a GDPR legal determination
